description: Require users to be authenticated before they can add or remove reactions on comments. This involves adding auth guards to the backend reaction endpoints and updating the frontend to only show reaction UI to logged-in users.

tasks.task:
Add authentication requirement to reaction endpoints in src/comments/comments.controller.ts:
1. Add @UseGuards(AuthGuard) decorator to the addReaction method (POST /comments/:commentId/reactions)
2. Add @UseGuards(AuthGuard) decorator to the removeReaction method (DELETE /comments/:commentId/reactions)
3. Modify addReaction to extract the user from the request (via @Req() decorator) instead of accepting it from the body - use req.user.name as the reaction user
4. Modify removeReaction to extract the user from the request instead of query params - use req.user.name instead of the 'user' query parameter
5. The getReactions endpoint (GET) should remain public so users can view reactions without auth

Update CommentSection.tsx to only allow authenticated users to react:
1. Only show the 'React' button (reaction-toggle-button) when user is logged in (user !== null)
2. Update handleReactionToggle to use user.name instead of the currentUser fallback - since reactions require auth, we know user will exist when this is called
3. Ensure the Authorization header is always sent with reaction requests (it's already conditionally added, but now it's required)
4. The reaction badges displaying existing reactions should remain visible to all users

example_frontend: When not logged in, navigate to a post with comments - verify the 'React' button is NOT visible on comments
When not logged in, verify existing reaction counts/badges are still visible on comments
Log in with valid credentials, navigate to a post with comments - verify the 'React' button IS now visible
While logged in, click 'React' on a comment, select an emoji - verify the reaction is added and your username is associated with it
While logged in, click on a reaction you already made - verify the reaction is removed
Log out and verify the 'React' button disappears but reaction counts remain visible
